topic: Warlock Overview
nugget: The Warlock is the 8th character introduced to Diablo II: Resurrected, released with the Reign of the Warlock expansion more than 25 years after the game's initial launch. The Warlock class manipulates Demons to their will, imbues its weapons with potent Magic damage, reigns F...
title_path: Warlock Overview

topic: Warlock Overview
nugget: That said, most Warlocks worth their salt utilize the Demon skill tree in some capacity to grant powerful buffs and even Auras to their main damage skill: Consume is generally used on the Warlock's native minions to improve Physical , Fire , or Magic damage, in addition to the...
title_path: Warlock Overview

topic: Warlock Overview
nugget: Another noteworthy part of the Warlock's kit is Blade Warp , a movement ability available as early as level 18. It's no Teleport , but it does allow you to traverse faster and move through passable walls, barred gates, and doorways.
title_path: Warlock Overview

topic: Warlock Overview
nugget: Early gearing priority: stack +skills first (it scales your core damage/utility), then cover key breakpoints (e.g., cast speed) and survivability.
title_path: Warlock Overview
Gearing Priorities

topic: Warlock Summoner Hell A1: Insight vs Spirit (main-hand scheme)
nugget: For Warlock (Reign of the Warlock), the question 'Insight vs Spirit' for a lvl~69 ladder Summoner in Hell A1 is primarily a MAIN-HAND scheme comparison (not just off-hand). With the official passive allowing a 2H weapon to be equipped in one hand while still using an off-hand (shield/Grimoire), Insight-as-mainhand becomes viable without giving up off-hand. Default recommendation: prefer Spirit (mainhand) for +skills/feel/survivability synergy with summoner; consider Insight mainhand mainly when mana sustain is the bottleneck. To finalize, clarify whether Spirit is sword or shield, and whether off-hand is shield or Grimoire.
title_path: Warlock gear decision
